title: Viz registry — block_type → component mapping + colour-purpose policy
description: A6 consults this to map A5 block_types to render components, to look up the colour palette + colour-purpose policy, and to pick layout templates per report_type. Phase-1 finding fold-in: colour-purpose registry — peach is reserved for partial-period/data-freshness flags ONLY; editorial colour (red=bad, green=good) is forbidden everywhere.

block_to_component.description: Map of A5 block_type to A6 component selection.

layouts.description: Per-report_type layout templates: ordering of sections, chip-row placement, footer composition.

alternate_components.description: Components A6 may pick from when context overrides (e.g. for ranked_table with delta intent, use diverging_bar instead of bar_chart_table_pair).

always_pair_table_with_chart.description: True for chartable blocks — chart and table both render. Per A6 spec: never one without the other for chartable blocks.

identity_palette.description: Hex colours assigned by rank order for categorical series. Consistent across charts in the same report.

allowed_palette.description: Explicit allow-list of every colour value (identity blue scale + neutrals + grey, plus structural keywords like transparent/none/currentColor/inherit) that A6 may emit anywhere. assertPaletteAllowed enforces it. The partial-period peach is NOT in this list — it is allowed ONLY on .partial-tagged nodes (see partial_only_colour).

partial_only_colour.description: The peach (partial-period / data-freshness) colour. Permitted ONLY on nodes tagged .partial; assertPaletteAllowed throws if it appears on any non-.partial node.

sentinel.description: Hex code for sentinel rows/bars (italic + muted).

partial_period.description: Hex code (peach) reserved for partial-period chips, dashed segments, hatched fills. NEVER repurposed.

forbidden_uses.description: Explicit ban list. Schema enforces by rejecting any visual_encoding that names these as a colour purpose.

supports_step_narration.description: True when this layout displays step_rationale (the section's 'Why this step' logic box) per section (staged-investigation reports).
